clusters.synthesis: These candidates all describe securing agentic systems: governance, tool-call side effects, delegated authorization, agentic software development security, and the broader attack/defense balance.

These candidates focus on evaluation and verification loops for agents, including verifier loops, trajectory evaluation, rollout-based iteration, skill harnesses, and broader agent development cycles.
These candidates frame software delivery as an end-to-end factory motion, including project-based delivery tied to strategic objectives.
These candidates both center on durable memory layers for reusable queries and graph-based recall instead of stateless retrieval.
This candidate treats the web stack as the agent-facing substrate, using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript as the interface for agent-generated video.
These candidates describe shared context substrates for enterprise agents: a common brain, workflow-guided agents, semantic ingestion, and systems of context.
These candidates focus on shaping runtime behavior through environment choice, isolation, persistence, prompting context, routing, and policy constraints.
These candidates emphasize knowledge organization, provenance, and entity-level grounding for systems that retrieve, connect, and explain information.
This candidate describes an autonomous multi-agent research loop that runs experiments and submits work after a quality gate.
This candidate is about multimodal routing and QA for an image agent, but not clearly enough to merge with a stronger existing topic, so it remains a singleton proto-topic.
This candidate is about GUI benchmark structure and multi-cursor computer use, which fits the browser-and-UI automation substrate rather than a broader web concept.
These candidates concern sandbox infrastructure for agent work, including always-on cloud harnesses and runtime isolation.
Turning implicit system knowledge into explicit written context for security analysis.
User and product feedback that shapes the next round of training data and model behavior.
How to remediate vulnerable dependencies across many repositories while keeping PRs reviewable and safe.
The gap between agents that can take actions and agents that can be trusted to finish messy work end to end.
The idea that agent systems should be organized into stable and volatile layers with different lifetimes.
A teaching approach for code changes that starts with system background and intuition before details.
Using observability data as input for autonomous debugging and repair loops
Human review after the agent assembles evidence and proposes a fix
How to adapt prompting, tools, and harnesses to a new model class with more latent capability.
Prompting agents by explaining the desired outcome and context rather than micromanaging the steps.
The interface defined by natural-language instructions, code constraints, and evaluative examples.
